entries.title: Semi-supervised knowledge transfer for deep learning from private training data
entries.why: Introduced PATE, a practical way to train models with differential-privacy guarantees, anchoring a decade of private machine learning.

entries.title: Proximal Policy Optimization
entries.why: Simple, stable policy-gradient method that became the field's default algorithm, later powering robotics and RLHF for language models.

entries.title: Preparing for malicious uses of AI
entries.why: Landmark cross-lab report anticipating AI misuse in security and politics; set the agenda for dual-use AI policy.

entries.title: AI safety via debate
entries.why: Proposed AI-vs-AI debate judged by humans, founding the scalable-oversight agenda for supervising smarter-than-human systems.

entries.title: Better language models and their implications
entries.why: GPT-2's staged release over misuse fears forced the field's first real debate about publishing powerful models.

entries.title: SuperGLUE: A Stickier Benchmark for General-Purpose Language Understanding Systems
entries.why: Set the standard language-understanding benchmark; models saturated it within a year, exposing how fast benchmarks go stale.

entries.title: Grandmaster level in StarCraft II using multi-agent reinforcement learning
entries.why: AlphaStar's league self-play reached grandmaster StarCraft II, showing RL could master long-horizon, partially observable real-time strategy.

entries.title: Scaling laws for neural language models
entries.why: Showed loss falls predictably with model size, data, and compute, turning scaling into a plannable science.

entries.title: Zoom In: An Introduction to Circuits
entries.why: Founded the circuits paradigm, making features and circuits the basic units for reverse-engineering neural networks.

entries.title: Language models are few-shot learners
entries.why: GPT-3 proved sheer scale yields few-shot learning from prompts alone, launching the large language model era.

entries.title: Bootstrap your own latent: A new approach to self-supervised Learning
entries.why: Made self-supervised pretraining work without negative pairs, simplifying and reshaping how visual representations are learned.

entries.title: Artificial Intelligence, Values, and Alignment
entries.why: Reframed alignment as a political question — whose values, chosen how — founding the normative debate around AI.

entries.title: Generative language modeling for automated theorem proving
entries.why: First showed a generative model could prove formal theorems, launching the neural theorem-proving line behind today's math models.

entries.title: Extracting Training Data from Large Language Models
entries.why: Showed GPT-2 leaks memorized personal data verbatim, founding the study of training-data extraction attacks on language models.

entries.title: Mastering Atari, Go, chess and shogi by planning with a learned model
entries.why: MuZero planned with a learned model, mastering games without being told the rules and uniting model-based and model-free RL.

entries.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
entries.why: CLIP learned vision from raw image-text pairs, enabling zero-shot recognition and becoming the backbone of later multimodal systems.

entries.title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
entries.why: Proved guided diffusion beats GANs, making diffusion the standard engine for image, video and audio generation.

entries.title: Evaluating large language models trained on code
entries.why: Introduced the pass@k metric and functional code-correctness testing, becoming the default benchmark for code-generation models for years.

entries.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
entries.why: Solved a 50-year challenge, predicting protein 3D structures from sequence at near-experimental accuracy and transforming biology.

entries.title: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
entries.why: DALL-E showed one transformer over text and image tokens can draw almost anything described in words.

entries.title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits
entries.why: Created the standard mathematical language for reverse-engineering transformers, the foundation of modern mechanistic interpretability.

entries.title: WebGPT: Improving the factual accuracy of language models through web browsing
entries.why: Showed a language model could use browsing tools to answer questions, seeding the modern tool-using agent.

entries.title: Magnetic control of tokamak plasmas through deep reinforcement learning
entries.why: First to control fusion plasma shapes in a real tokamak with RL, showing AI can steer physical experiments.

entries.title: In-Context Learning and Induction Heads
entries.why: Linked a specific circuit, induction heads, to in-context learning, showing mechanisms can explain emergent model abilities.

entries.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
entries.why: InstructGPT made reinforcement learning from human feedback the standard recipe for turning raw language models into aligned assistants.

entries.title: Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models
entries.why: Named and documented capabilities that appear abruptly with scale, framing years of debate over what scaling buys.

entries.title: Red Teaming Language Models to Reduce Harms: Methods, Scaling Behaviors, and Lessons Learned
entries.why: Turned red-teaming into a systematic, measurable discipline, publishing methods and data that labs now treat as standard.

entries.title: Toy Models of Superposition
entries.why: Explained why neurons mix many concepts at once, reframing the field's core problem and motivating sparse autoencoders.

entries.title: Discovering faster matrix multiplication algorithms with reinforcement learning
entries.why: AlphaTensor beat Strassen's 50-year-old matrix multiplication record, opening RL as an engine for discovering new algorithms.

entries.title: Competition-level code generation with AlphaCode
entries.why: Reached average-human level in programming competitions, first public proof language models could reason through unseen problems.

entries.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
entries.why: Replaced human harm labels with AI self-critique against written principles, making alignment more scalable and transparent.

entries.title: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations
entries.why: Showed models can auto-generate evaluation datasets at scale, surfacing behaviors like sycophancy and making behavioral testing cheap.

entries.title: GPT-4
entries.why: Its performance was forecast from runs using 10,000x less compute, proving scaling laws can steer frontier training.

entries.title: Model evaluation for extreme risks
entries.why: Made dangerous-capability evaluations the core frontier safety paradigm, underpinning labs' scaling and preparedness frameworks.

entries.title: Improving mathematical reasoning with process supervision
entries.why: Showed rewarding each reasoning step beats rewarding only answers, making process supervision the standard recipe for training reasoners.

entries.title: Large language models encode clinical knowledge
entries.why: Demonstrated large language models can answer medical questions at near-expert level, launching the medical LLM research wave.

entries.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning
entries.why: Showed sparse autoencoders unmix superposed activations into interpretable features, launching the SAE era of interpretability.

entries.title: Collective Constitutional AI: Aligning a Language Model with Public Input
entries.why: First experiment letting the public write a model's guiding constitution, launching participatory approaches to AI governance.

entries.title: Learning skillful medium-range global weather forecasting
entries.why: Showed a machine-learning model could beat the best physics-based systems at medium-range global weather forecasting, in seconds.

entries.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization: Eliciting Strong Capabilities With Weak Supervision
entries.why: Showed weak supervisors can partially elicit strong models' capabilities, giving superalignment its first practical experimental testbed.

entries.title: Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models
entries.why: FunSearch produced the first genuinely new mathematical discoveries from a language model, finding knowledge beyond its training data.

entries.title: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
entries.why: Showed deceptive backdoor behavior can survive standard safety training, launching model-organisms-of-misalignment research.

entries.title: Video generation models as world simulators
entries.why: Sora scaled diffusion transformers to minute-long coherent video, recasting video generation as learned world simulation.

entries.title: The Instruction Hierarchy: Training LLMs to Prioritize Privileged Instructions
entries.why: Defined privileged instruction levels so models resist prompt injection, becoming the standard defense blueprint for deployed assistants.

entries.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet
entries.why: Proved feature extraction scales to frontier models, finding millions of interpretable, steerable features including safety-relevant ones.

entries.title: Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3
entries.why: Extended structure prediction from proteins to DNA, RNA, ligands and complexes, modeling how molecules interact across life.

entries.title: GPTs are GPTs: Labor market impact potential of LLMs
entries.why: Quantified how far LLMs reach into nearly every occupation, anchoring global debates on AI and jobs.

entries.title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified
entries.why: Became the standard benchmark for autonomous coding agents, setting how every lab measures real software-task performance.

entries.title: Learning to reason with LLMs
entries.why: Introduced o1: RL on chains of thought plus test-time compute scaling, the paradigm every reasoning model follows.

entries.title: Developing a computer use model
entries.why: First frontier agent to operate a computer like a person, clicking and typing, launching the screen-operating agent era.

entries.title: Quantum error correction below the surface code threshold
entries.why: Demonstrated quantum error correction below the surface-code threshold, a key milestone toward scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computers.

entries.title: Alignment faking in large language models
entries.why: First evidence of a model strategically complying during training to preserve its existing preferences, without being taught to.

entries.title: Introducing deep research
entries.why: Created the autonomous research-agent category, running long multi-step web investigations end-to-end, and was quickly copied industry-wide.

entries.title: Constitutional Classifiers: Defending against universal jailbreaks
entries.why: First deployed safeguard to withstand thousands of hours of universal-jailbreak red-teaming, shifting defenses to layered classifiers.

entries.title: Training large language models on narrow tasks can lead to broad misalignment
entries.why: Found fine-tuning on narrowly bad data, like insecure code, triggers broad misalignment, revealing how fragile learned values are.

entries.title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
entries.why: Traced step-by-step internal computations with attribution graphs, revealing planning and shared circuitry inside a production model.

entries.title: Reasoning models don't always say what they think
entries.why: Showed chains of thought often misrepresent models' real reasoning, tempering hopes for CoT-based safety monitoring.

entries.title: AlphaGenome: advancing regulatory variant effect prediction with a unified DNA sequence model
entries.why: Unified diverse genome-function predictions in one DNA model, advancing how researchers interpret regulatory variants across the genome.

entries.title: Subliminal Learning: Language Models Transmit Behavioral Traits via Hidden Signals in Data
entries.why: Showed models trained on another model's outputs inherit hidden traits, reshaping views of distillation and synthetic data.

entries.title: Why language models hallucinate
entries.why: Argued benchmarks reward confident guessing over honest uncertainty, reframing hallucination as an evaluation-design problem and prompting scoring reform.

entries.title: Olympiad-level formal mathematical reasoning with reinforcement learning
entries.why: AlphaProof reached silver-medal standard on International Math Olympiad problems, a landmark for reinforcement learning in formal mathematics.

entries.title: GDPVAL: Evaluating AI Model Performance on Real-World Economically Valuable Tasks
entries.why: Shifted evaluation toward real-world, expert-graded professional tasks, moving past academic benchmarks toward measuring economically useful work.
